title: Embeddings & RAG: Searching by Meaning
summary: How does a chatbot answer questions about your documents? Usually not by training on them, but by turning meaning into vectors and letting the model answer with retrieved text in front of it.

context.headline: An open-book exam for AI
context.description: RAG - retrieval-augmented generation - is a common way AI answers from documents it was never trained on. The question is converted into a vector, similar passages are found in a vector database, and the model answers with those passages in front of it. This pattern is widely used in company knowledge bots and document assistants.
context.when: Behind almost every 'chat with your documents' product
